A single container on apexalgo-iad (us-east-1) serves both the Hono API backend and static React frontend through the existing Cloudflare Tunnel. The app provides real-time arrivals, configurable favorites, transfer intelligence, alerts, trip tracking, commute analytics, accessibility features, and system health visualization.\n\nThe value proposition is speed-to-information (favorites-first, data in under 3 seconds), transfer intelligence (real-time \"should I transfer?\" analysis), and alerts that are actually useful (plain-language, filtered to YOUR stations).\n\n## Architecture Decision\nSingle container on apexalgo-iad was chosen over Cloudflare Workers or Fly.io because:\n- Zero new infra cost (rides on existing cluster)\n- us-east-1 gives sub-10ms latency to MTA feeds (NYC-hosted)\n- Cloudflare Tunnel already configured for public HTTPS access\n- GitOps deployment via ArgoCD follows existing patterns\n- Same-origin eliminates CORS entirely\n\n## Progress\n- [ ] Phase 1: Core — Real-time arrivals, favorites, search, onboarding, complex mapping\n- [ ] Phase 2: Smart Commute — Transfer analysis, commute presets, express/local\n- [ ] Phase 3: Alerts & Notifications — Alert simplification, push notifications\n- [ ] Phase 4: Polish — Offline, accessibility, performance, error states\n- [ ] Phase 5: Intelligence — Predictive delays, trip tracking, context switching, journal\n- [ ] Phase 6: Awareness — Elevator status, walking comparison, OMNY, train diagram, health dashboard, annual summary\n- [ ] Phase 7: Trust & Resilience — Shuttle bus info, data freshness\n- [ ] Cross-cutting: Testing, security, data migration, observability\nLabels: umbrella","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8b21fe37-162a-4065-bd6c-aa7110aae3bc","uuid":"558c0ced-2492-47eb-a65e-5574a6dfd4d7","timestamp":"2026-07-03T19:01:32.547Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"ID: mta-my-way-s23\nTitle: Genesis: MTA My Way Implementation\nStatus: open\nPriority: P0\nType: genesis\nDescription: ## Genesis Bead\nTied to plan: /home/coding/mta-my-way/docs/plan/plan.md\n\n## Overview\nMTA My Way is a mobile-first PWA for NYC subway commuters. A single container on apexalgo-iad (us-east-1) serves both the Hono API backend and static React frontend through the existing Cloudflare Tunnel. The app provides real-time arrivals, configurable favorites, transfer intelligence, alerts, trip tracking, commute analytics, accessibility features, and system health visualization.\n\nThe value proposition is speed-to-information (favorites-first, data in under 3 seconds), transfer intelligence (real-time \"should I transfer?\" analysis), and alerts that are actually useful (plain-language, filtered to YOUR stations).\n\n## Architecture Decision\nSingle container on apexalgo-iad was chosen over Cloudflare Workers or Fly.io because:\n- Zero new infra cost (rides on existing cluster)\n- us-east-1 gives sub-10ms latency to MTA feeds (NYC-hosted)\n- Cloudflare Tunnel already configured for public HTTPS access\n- GitOps deployment via ArgoCD follows existing patterns\n- Same-origin eliminates CORS entirely\n\n## Progress\n- [ ] Phase 1: Core — Real-time arrivals, favorites, search, onboarding, complex mapping\n- [ ] Phase 2: Smart Commute — Transfer analysis, commute presets, express/local\n- [ ] Phase 3: Alerts & Notifications — Alert simplification, push notifications\n- [ ] Phase 4: Polish — Offline, accessibility, performance, error states\n- [ ] Phase 5: Intelligence — Predictive delays, trip tracking, context switching, journal\n- [ ] Phase 6: Awareness — Elevator status, walking comparison, OMNY, train diagram, health dashboard, annual summary\n- [ ] Phase 7: Trust & Resilience — Shuttle bus info, data freshness\n- [ ] Cross-cutting: Testing, security, data migration, observability\nLabels: umbrella","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}
